refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct obligations: (1) deriving requirements from context, (2) recording them, and (3) managing them over time. Derivation is the primary functional obligation in the problem domain; recording and managing are implementation concerns that belong in the DP/IM layers. The refined FR focuses on the core functional need: translating mission context into measurable performance targets. This remains solution-neutral and avoids prescribing how derivation, storage, or lifecycle management are realized.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Demonstrate that given a worksite mission context (e.g., site layout, production targets, safety constraints), the system produces a documented set of performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) that are traceable to input parameters and accepted by the Site Manager and Safety Manager.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The NGHE solution shall accept worksite mission context inputs (e.g., site layout, production targets, safety constraints, operational policies).
CR-NGHE-005.2: The NGHE solution shall compute performance requirements (e.g., cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) from worksite mission context.
CR-NGHE-005.3: The NGHE solution shall record derived performance requirements in a retrievable format.
CR-NGHE-005.4: The NGHE solution shall maintain a history of performance requirement baselines and calibration updates.
CR-NGHE-005.5: The NGHE solution shall enable traceability between worksite inputs and derived performance requirements.
refined_fr.note: The original FR is not atomic; it has been decomposed into five atomic sub-FRs. CR-NGHE-005 itself now serves as a parent functional requirement. Each sub-FR addresses a single obligation and remains solution-neutral.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement a mission-context capture module that accepts worksite operational parameters (mission type, environmental conditions, safety constraints) as structured inputs and outputs a normalized performance requirement specification document with traceability metadata.

DP-B: Establish a parameterized performance baseline library indexed by worksite mission class, with a lookup-and-adapt mechanism that retrieves baseline targets and applies worksite-specific modifiers (safety margins, environmental factors) to derive final performance requirements.

DP-C: Deploy a real-time performance telemetry aggregator that continuously monitors worksite operational state and automatically adjusts performance targets via feedback control, with versioned snapshots stored as baseline history.

DP-D: Create a requirements derivation engine that accepts worksite safety plan and operational policy documents as unstructured or semi-structured inputs, applies rule-based inference to extract performance constraints, and outputs a traceable requirements matrix with source citations.

DP-E: Implement a hybrid approach combining structured mission-context input (DP-A) with policy-driven constraint extraction (DP-D), where derived requirements are validated against a safety/operational policy compliance checker before finalization and archival.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-E: Hybrid mission-context capture with policy-driven constraint extraction and compliance validation. Accepts worksite mission parameters and safety/operational policy documents; applies structured input processing (DP-A logic) and rule-based policy inference (DP-D logic); validates derived requirements against policy compliance rules; outputs documented performance targets with full traceability to mission inputs and policy sources; maintains versioned baseline history with calibration update audit trail.

potential_sources: DP-E couples mission-context input processing with policy inference—both are logically distinct but must share a common traceability framework. Mitigation: define a neutral traceability schema (source ID, derivation rule, timestamp) that decouples the input processing logic from the inference engine.

DP-E depends on external policy document format and structure. Risk: if policy documents are unstructured or inconsistent, rule-based extraction becomes brittle. Mitigation: require worksite policies to conform to a lightweight schema (e.g., structured templates or controlled vocabulary) as a precondition input.

Baseline history and calibration updates introduce temporal coupling. Risk: concurrent updates to baseline versions could create inconsistency. Mitigation: enforce immutable versioning with timestamp-ordered audit trail and single-writer semantics for baseline updates.
coupling_check.notes: DP-E avoids tight coupling to specific mission types or policy formats by treating them as parameterized inputs. The compliance validation step is logically independent and can be extended without affecting mission-context capture. Traceability metadata acts as a decoupling layer between input sources and derived requirements.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite safety plans and operational policies are available in a form that can be parsed or indexed (structured, semi-structured, or with human annotation). RISK: If policies are purely narrative or inconsistently documented, rule-based extraction will have low precision. MITIGATION: Establish a policy documentation standard as a precondition; provide templates or guidance to worksites.

ASSUMPTION: Mission-context parameters can be defined as a finite, enumerable set (e.g., mission type, environment class, hazard level). RISK: Novel or hybrid mission types may not fit predefined categories. MITIGATION: Design the mission-context schema with extensibility (e.g., custom parameter slots) and fallback to manual review for out-of-scope cases.

ASSUMPTION: Performance targets derived from mission context and policy are stable over the duration of a worksite deployment. RISK: If operational conditions change rapidly, baseline targets may become stale. MITIGATION: Define a baseline refresh trigger (e.g., policy update, mission scope change) and establish a review cadence.

ASSUMPTION: Traceability metadata (source citations, derivation rules) can be automatically captured and maintained. RISK: If traceability is manual or ad-hoc, audit trail will be incomplete. MITIGATION: Embed traceability capture in the derivation engine; make it non-optional.

ASSUMPTION: Compliance validation rules are deterministic and non-contradictory. RISK: Policy conflicts or ambiguous rules could block requirement finalization. MITIGATION: Implement conflict detection and escalation to human reviewer; document resolution decisions in audit trail.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine FR-NGHE-005 to explicitly separate two sub-requirements: (1) FR-NGHE-005a: Capture and normalize mission-context inputs into a structured performance requirement specification; (2) FR-NGHE-005b: Extract and validate performance constraints from worksite safety and operational policies, and reconcile with mission-derived targets. This decomposition clarifies the logical boundaries within DP-E and enables independent testing and evolution of mission-context processing vs. policy-driven constraint extraction. Additionally, add an acceptance criterion: 'The system shall maintain an immutable, timestamped audit trail of all baseline versions and calibration updates, with full traceability to source inputs and policy rules.' This ensures the history requirement is testable and architecturally explicit.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Mission context capture, policy document ingestion, constraint extraction, compliance validation, and performance target derivation with full traceability and versioned baseline management.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives worksite mission parameters and operational context from upstream planning/tasking authority.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Outputs compliance validation results, policy violations, and derived safety constraints to Safety Services for enforcement.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exports documented performance targets, traceability metadata, and baseline history for monitoring and audit.

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent fit. DP-E is fundamentally a mission-to-requirements translation layer that synthesizes policy and mission context into validated constraints. NGHE Solution is the natural home for structured input processing (DP-A), rule-based inference (DP-D), compliance validation, traceability, and versioned baseline management. Minor diffusion: performance targets and compliance alerts must flow to Safety Services and Telemetry, but these are clean output interfaces, not internal logic leakage.

Poor fit. Safety Services is a consumer of compliance validation outputs, not the producer. DP-E's core logic—mission context capture, policy inference, and traceability—belongs upstream in requirements derivation, not in runtime safety enforcement. Placing DP-E here would scatter mission-to-constraint translation across Safety Services, creating tight coupling and diffusing policy interpretation logic.

Very poor fit. Vehicle is a physical/operational platform, not a policy or requirements processing layer. DP-E involves document ingestion, rule inference, and compliance validation—none of which belong in vehicle control or state management. Placing DP-E here would create severe diffusion and architectural inversion.

Very poor fit. Teleoperation is a command/control interface for real-time operator interaction. DP-E is a static/batch mission-context and policy processing layer that runs before or parallel to teleoperation. No logical cohesion; placing DP-E here would diffuse policy logic into operator control pathways.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: DP-E (hybrid mission-context capture with policy-driven constraint extraction and compliance validation) is a requirements-derivation and policy-interpretation layer that logically belongs in the NGHE Solution module. NGHE Solution is the designated home for structured input processing (DP-A) and rule-based inference (DP-D), both of which are core to DP-E. The module can cleanly encapsulate mission parameter ingestion, policy document parsing, constraint extraction, compliance rule evaluation, traceability tracking, and baseline versioning. Output interfaces (SafetyAlert to Safety Services, Telemetry for audit trail) are well-defined and do not require internal logic to diffuse.
containment_risks: Policy document schema evolution: If policy formats or compliance rule structures change frequently, DP-E may require frequent updates. Mitigation: establish a policy schema versioning strategy within NGHE Solution.

Traceability metadata explosion: Maintaining full traceability from mission inputs through policy inference to derived targets could create large metadata overhead. Mitigation: implement selective traceability logging and archive old baselines.

Coupling to Safety Services: DP-E outputs compliance alerts to Safety Services. If Safety Services requirements change, DP-E validation logic may need adjustment. Mitigation: define a stable SafetyAlert interface contract.

Mission parameter variability: If worksite mission parameters are highly heterogeneous, DP-A (structured input processing) may struggle with normalization. Mitigation: establish mission parameter templates and validation schemas upfront.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: HIGH. FR-CR-NGHE-005 requires documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs with baseline history. IM boundary (NGHE Solution) explicitly owns mission context capture, constraint extraction, compliance validation, and versioned baseline management. Acceptance criteria are directly verifiable: (1) performance targets exist and are documented; (2) traceability metadata links targets to mission inputs and policy sources; (3) baseline history with calibration audit trail is maintained. All three are containable within NGHE Solution scope.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: CLEAR. NGHE Solution is the unambiguous owner of requirements derivation from mission context and policy. External interfaces (Tasking in, SafetyAlert and Telemetry out) are well-defined and do not create ownership ambiguity. No competing modules claim this responsibility.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-E (hybrid mission-context capture with policy-driven constraint extraction and compliance validation) directly addresses FR-CR-NGHE-005. DP-E explicitly includes: (1) mission parameter acceptance and policy document ingestion (addresses 'derive from worksite mission context'); (2) structured input processing (DP-A) and rule-based policy inference (DP-D) (addresses 'policy-driven constraint extraction'); (3) compliance validation (addresses 'compliance validation'); (4) documented performance targets with full traceability (addresses 'documented set of performance targets traceable to worksite inputs'); (5) versioned baseline history with calibration audit trail (addresses 'maintains history of baselines/calibration updates'). DP-E is complete, logically coherent, and satisfies all acceptance criteria.

diffusion_risks: POLICY SCHEMA EVOLUTION: Policy document formats and compliance rule structures may change. Mitigation: establish policy schema versioning and backward-compatibility strategy within NGHE Solution to prevent diffusion of policy interpretation logic into Safety Services or Vehicle modules.

TRACEABILITY METADATA OVERHEAD: Full traceability from mission inputs through policy inference to derived targets could create large metadata volumes. Mitigation: implement selective traceability logging (log critical decision points, not all intermediate states) and archive old baselines to external storage to keep NGHE Solution memory footprint bounded.

SAFETY SERVICES COUPLING: DP-E outputs compliance alerts to Safety Services via SafetyAlert interface. If Safety Services requirements change (e.g., new alert types, severity levels), DP-E validation logic may require updates. Mitigation: define a stable, versioned SafetyAlert interface contract and isolate compliance rule evaluation from alert formatting.

MISSION PARAMETER NORMALIZATION: If worksite mission parameters are highly heterogeneous (e.g., different coordinate systems, units, mission types), DP-A (structured input processing) may struggle with normalization and validation. Mitigation: establish mission parameter templates, validation schemas, and input canonicalization rules upfront; document supported mission parameter profiles.

BASELINE VERSIONING COMPLEXITY: Managing versioned baselines with calibration audit trails requires careful state management. Risk: if baseline versioning logic is not cleanly encapsulated, it may diffuse into Safety Services or Telemetry consumers. Mitigation: implement baseline versioning as a cohesive internal service within NGHE Solution; expose only stable query/retrieval interfaces to external consumers.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: EXCELLENT CONTAINMENT (0.92 score). DP-E is fundamentally a mission-to-requirements translation and policy-interpretation layer. NGHE Solution is the natural and only appropriate home for this logic. All core responsibilities (mission context capture, policy document ingestion, constraint extraction, compliance validation, traceability, baseline versioning) are logically cohesive and belong together. Output interfaces (SafetyAlert, Telemetry) are clean, unidirectional, and do not require internal logic to leak into downstream modules. No competing modules (Safety Services, Vehicle, Teleoperation) have legitimate claims to DP-E ownership. Minor diffusion risk exists only in interface stability and metadata management, both of which are manageable through internal design discipline.

recommended_revisions: POLICY SCHEMA VERSIONING: Add explicit requirement to NGHE Solution design: 'Maintain a policy schema registry with version tracking. Support at least N-1 prior policy schema versions to enable graceful migration and prevent breaking changes to Safety Services or Telemetry consumers.'

TRACEABILITY INTERFACE CONTRACT: Define a formal Traceability interface that NGHE Solution exports to Telemetry. Specify: (1) what metadata is logged (mission input ID, policy rule ID, derived constraint ID, timestamp); (2) retention policy (e.g., keep last 100 baselines, archive older ones); (3) query API (e.g., 'get traceability for constraint X'). This prevents diffusion of traceability logic into Telemetry.

SAFETY ALERT INTERFACE VERSIONING: Define SafetyAlert as a versioned, stable interface. Specify alert schema (alert type, severity, policy rule violated, derived constraint affected, timestamp). Document backward compatibility strategy. This prevents DP-E validation logic from becoming tightly coupled to Safety Services implementation changes.

MISSION PARAMETER PROFILE DOCUMENTATION: Create a mission parameter profile specification document that defines: (1) supported mission types (e.g., inspection, delivery, surveillance); (2) required and optional parameters for each type; (3) valid value ranges, units, and coordinate systems; (4) validation rules. This reduces diffusion risk from heterogeneous mission inputs.

BASELINE VERSIONING INTERNAL SERVICE: Encapsulate baseline versioning as an internal service within NGHE Solution. Expose only: (1) 'create_baseline(performance_targets, mission_inputs, policy_sources)' → baseline_id; (2) 'get_baseline(baseline_id)' → baseline_record; (3) 'list_baselines(filters)' → baseline_summary_list. Do not expose internal versioning state or calibration audit trail directly; wrap in query methods to prevent external modules from depending on internal structure.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-IM, FR-DP, DP-IM) pass with high confidence. FR-CR-NGHE-005 is well-defined, DP-E is logically complete and directly addresses all acceptance criteria, and NGHE Solution is the unambiguous owner with excellent containment (0.92 score). However, the DP-IM gate identifies five concrete diffusion risks that require architectural decomposition to mitigate: (1) policy schema evolution, (2) traceability metadata overhead, (3) safety services coupling, (4) mission parameter normalization, and (5) baseline versioning complexity. These risks are not showstoppers but indicate that DP-E is sufficiently complex to warrant sub-level decomposition. Deriving next-level sub-FRs will make these risks explicit, allocate ownership of mitigation strategies, and enable detailed design to proceed with clear architectural boundaries. Given high safety criticality, proactive decomposition is justified.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-CR-NGHE-005-A: Ingest and normalize mission parameters from worksite tasking inputs, validate against supported mission parameter profiles, and canonicalize into internal representation.
FR-CR-NGHE-005-B: Ingest policy documents, parse policy rules, and maintain a versioned policy schema registry with backward-compatibility support for at least N-1 prior versions.

FR-CR-NGHE-005-C: Extract and derive performance constraints from mission parameters and policy rules using rule-based inference, and validate derived constraints for logical consistency and safety compliance.

FR-CR-NGHE-005-D: Create, version, and manage performance target baselines with full traceability metadata (mission input ID, policy rule ID, derived constraint ID, timestamp) and maintain calibration audit trail.

FR-CR-NGHE-005-E: Query and retrieve baseline records and traceability metadata via stable, versioned interfaces (Traceability interface) without exposing internal versioning state to external consumers.

FR-CR-NGHE-005-F: Generate and emit compliance validation alerts (SafetyAlert) when derived constraints violate policy rules, using a stable, versioned alert schema with backward-compatibility guarantees.

revise.why: DP-E is logically sound but requires architectural refinement to address diffusion risks. Recommend decomposing DP-E into six sub-DPs corresponding to the six sub-FRs above: (1) DP-E-A: Mission Parameter Normalization Engine (with profile validation and canonicalization); (2) DP-E-B: Policy Schema Registry and Versioning Service (with backward-compatibility layer); (3) DP-E-C: Constraint Derivation and Validation Engine (rule-based inference with consistency checking); (4) DP-E-D: Baseline Versioning and Audit Trail Service (with selective traceability logging); (5) DP-E-E: Traceability Query Interface (stable, versioned API); (6) DP-E-F: Compliance Alert Formatter (with versioned SafetyAlert schema). This decomposition makes each diffusion risk addressable at the sub-DP level and enables clear ownership of mitigation strategies.

assumptions: Policy document formats and compliance rule structures are expected to evolve over the system lifetime; backward compatibility is a design requirement, not optional.
Mission parameters from worksite tasking inputs are heterogeneous and require normalization; a mission parameter profile specification can be established upfront to bound this heterogeneity.
Traceability metadata volumes are expected to be large; selective logging (critical decision points only) is acceptable and preferred over exhaustive logging.

Safety Services and Telemetry consumers are external to NGHE Solution and must not depend on internal versioning state or audit trail structure; all access must be through stable, versioned interfaces.

Baseline versioning and calibration audit trail management are safety-critical and must be encapsulated within NGHE Solution to prevent external modules from corrupting or misinterpreting baseline state.
open_risks: POLICY SCHEMA EVOLUTION: If policy schema versioning is not implemented with clear backward-compatibility rules, Safety Services and Telemetry consumers may break when policy formats change. Mitigation: define policy schema versioning strategy and N-1 compatibility requirement in DP-E-B design.

TRACEABILITY METADATA EXPLOSION: If all intermediate states are logged, metadata volumes could exceed storage and query performance budgets. Mitigation: implement selective traceability logging in DP-E-D; define retention policy and archival strategy.

SAFETY ALERT INTERFACE COUPLING: If SafetyAlert schema is not versioned and stabilized, changes to Safety Services requirements could force DP-E-F to be redesigned. Mitigation: define SafetyAlert as a formal, versioned interface contract in DP-E-F design.

MISSION PARAMETER HETEROGENEITY: If worksite mission parameters are more heterogeneous than anticipated, DP-E-A normalization logic may become complex and fragile. Mitigation: establish mission parameter profile specification upfront; document supported profiles and validation rules.

BASELINE VERSIONING STATE LEAKAGE: If baseline versioning logic is not cleanly encapsulated, external modules may depend on internal state structure, creating tight coupling. Mitigation: expose only stable query/retrieval interfaces (DP-E-E) and hide internal versioning state.
evidence_needed: Mission parameter profile specification document: define supported mission types, required/optional parameters, valid value ranges, units, coordinate systems, and validation rules.
Policy schema versioning strategy: define schema version numbering, backward-compatibility rules, migration procedures, and N-1 support commitment.
Traceability metadata specification: define what metadata is logged (mission input ID, policy rule ID, derived constraint ID, timestamp), retention policy, and query API.
SafetyAlert interface contract: define alert schema (alert type, severity, policy rule violated, derived constraint affected, timestamp) and backward-compatibility strategy.
Baseline versioning internal service design: specify create_baseline(), get_baseline(), list_baselines() APIs and internal state management without exposing versioning details.
Proof-of-concept or prototype for mission parameter normalization and policy rule inference to validate that DP-E-A and DP-E-C can handle expected heterogeneity and complexity.
